explanation: Detour signs guide drivers along a temporary route when the normal road cannot be used.

text: What instruction is given by this road sign?
options: Parking is prohibited, but stopping is allowed
Stopping and parking are prohibited
Only emergency vehicles may stop here
Stopping is permitted for loading only
explanation: This regulatory sign means that neither stopping nor parking is permitted.

text: What do the red and white diagonal stripes on this barrier indicate?
options: The road is blocked or there is a dangerous obstruction ahead
A parking area begins ahead
A reduced speed zone starts here
Traffic must merge into another lane
explanation: Red and white striped barriers warn drivers of an obstruction or a closed section of road.

text: What information is conveyed by this temporary yellow sign with a black arrow?
options: The normal route continues ahead
Traffic is being diverted or shifted because of temporary road conditions
A maximum speed limit applies ahead
A tourist attraction is nearby
explanation: Yellow signs are temporary signs used during roadworks or other temporary traffic changes.

text: What does this blue circular sign displaying a white car symbol mean?
options: Motor cars are prohibited
The route or lane is reserved for motor cars
Parking for motor cars is provided ahead
The road is intended mainly for passenger vehicles
explanation: Blue circular signs indicate mandatory instructions. This sign shows that the route is reserved for motor cars.

text: What does this priority sign indicate?
options: You have priority over oncoming traffic
The road narrows ahead
Overtaking is prohibited
You must give way to oncoming traffic
explanation: This regulatory sign indicates that you must yield to vehicles approaching from the opposite direction where the road is too narrow for both to pass safely.

text: What does a smaller STOP sign mounted below a larger STOP sign indicate?
options: A traffic circle lies ahead
All traffic approaching the intersection must stop
Traffic must stop for a scholar patrol when instructed
Pedestrians always have right of way
explanation: This sign combination is used at scholar patrol crossings. Drivers must stop when instructed to allow children to cross safely.

text: What information does this brown sign with a white telephone symbol provide?
options: A police station is nearby
An emergency telephone is available
A public hotel is ahead
Tourist information is available
explanation: Brown tourist information signs with a telephone symbol indicate the location of an emergency telephone.

text: What instruction does this blue circular sign with a white arrow pointing right give?
options: Turning right is recommended
You must turn right
The right lane ends ahead
You may turn right if it is safe
explanation: Blue circular signs give mandatory instructions. Drivers must turn right.

text: What is the purpose of a blue rectangular sign displaying the word 'Exit'?
options: It indicates the location of an exit from the current route
It marks a compulsory stopping point
It identifies a designated parking area
It indicates the beginning of a speed-restricted zone
explanation: Blue guidance signs are used to direct drivers to exits and other facilities.

text: What restriction does this regulatory sign place on road users?
options: Only agricultural vehicles may proceed
Tractors are prohibited from entering
A tractor crossing is ahead
The road is reserved for farm traffic
explanation: This regulatory sign prohibits tractors from entering or using the road.

text: What is the purpose of the dashed white lines shown in this turning lane?
options: They indicate that overtaking is permitted
They guide vehicles through the turning path
They mark the beginning of a speed-restricted area
They indicate a parking area
explanation: Dashed lane markings guide vehicles safely through intersections and turning lanes.

text: What restriction does this regulatory sign impose?
options: Left turns are permitted
Left turns are prohibited
Drivers must stop before turning left
Only buses may turn left
explanation: The red-bordered circle with a diagonal slash over a left-turn arrow prohibits left turns.

text: What information does this brown sign displaying a white 'i' provide?
options: Tourist information is available
A hospital is located nearby
Public parking is available ahead
A police station is nearby
explanation: Brown tourism signs with a white 'i' indicate a tourist information office or information point.

text: What does the solid white line along the edge of the roadway indicate?
options: It marks the edge of the roadway
It marks an overtaking lane
It indicates a designated parking area
It marks a compulsory stopping point
explanation: A solid white edge line defines the boundary of the roadway.

text: What does this blue circular sign displaying a white 'T' indicate?
options: Only cash payments are accepted ahead
There is no alternative route available
A toll road may be used if you choose
A toll gate lies ahead
explanation: This sign warns drivers that they are approaching a toll gate where toll fees are payable.

text: What does the solid white stop line at traffic lights indicate?
options: You must stop behind the line when the traffic signal is red
You may overtake at this point
Parking is permitted beyond the line
The road ends ahead
explanation: When the traffic signal is red, vehicles must stop behind the stop line.

text: What does this regulatory sign indicate?
options: Cyclists must use this route
Bicycles are prohibited
A bicycle crossing lies ahead
Only motorcycles are prohibited
explanation: The red-bordered sign with a bicycle crossed out prohibits bicycles from entering.

text: What information is provided by this green sign displaying '10 km'?
options: The remaining distance to a destination or point of interest
The maximum speed limit is 10 km/h
Parking is available in 10 kilometres
A compulsory stop is 10 kilometres ahead
explanation: Green guidance signs often display the remaining distance to destinations.

text: What do these white markings in a parking area indicate?
options: Individual parking bays
Traffic lanes through the parking area
A reduced speed zone
An area where parking is prohibited
explanation: The white painted lines define the boundaries of individual parking spaces.

text: What restriction does this regulatory sign place on road users?
options: Horse riders may use this route
Horses are prohibited
Horse-drawn vehicles have priority
A horse crossing lies ahead
explanation: This regulatory sign prohibits horses and horse riders from entering or using the road.

text: What information is provided by this brown tourism sign displaying a bed symbol?
options: A hospital is located nearby
Accommodation is available nearby
A public parking area is ahead
A police station is nearby
explanation: Brown tourism signs with a bed symbol indicate accommodation such as hotels, lodges or guest houses.

text: What instruction does this blue circular sign with a truck marked '10 t' give?
options: Construction vehicles over 10 tonnes must use this road
All heavy motor vehicles must use this road
Goods vehicles exceeding 10 tonnes must use this road
Only goods vehicles not exceeding 10 tonnes may use this road
explanation: This mandatory sign indicates that goods vehicles with a mass exceeding 10 tonnes must use this section of road.

text: What restriction does this regulatory sign impose?
options: Right turns are permitted
Right turns are prohibited
Drivers must stop before turning right
Only buses may turn right
explanation: The red-bordered circle with a diagonal slash over a right-turn arrow prohibits right turns.

text: What information is provided by this green guidance sign?
options: It shows the direction to a destination
It indicates the maximum speed limit
It marks a compulsory stopping point
It indicates a designated parking area
explanation: Green guidance signs provide directions and navigation information to destinations.

text: What does this regulatory sign indicate?
options: The use of a hooter is prohibited
You may sound your hooter if necessary
No hooter may be used for the next 500 metres
Drivers must switch off their engines
explanation: This regulatory sign prohibits the use of a hooter in the area.

text: What do broken yellow lines painted along the edge of the road indicate?
options: Parking is prohibited at all times
Parking or stopping is restricted under certain conditions
Overtaking is permitted
A reduced speed zone begins
explanation: Broken yellow edge lines indicate that parking or stopping is restricted according to the applicable road traffic rules.

text: What restriction does this regulatory sign place on road users?
options: Animal-drawn vehicles may use this road
Animal-drawn vehicles are prohibited
Only horse riders may proceed
A horse crossing lies ahead
explanation: This regulatory sign prohibits animal-drawn vehicles from entering or using the road.

text: What do these white road markings indicate?
options: A pedestrian crossing
A designated parking area
The beginning of a speed-restricted zone
A one-way street
explanation: The white road markings indicate a pedestrian crossing where pedestrians have right of way.

text: What restriction does this regulatory sign indicate?
options: Vehicles longer than 15 metres are prohibited
Vehicles higher than 15 metres may use this road
Only vehicles longer than 15 metres may use this road
Only vehicles not exceeding 15 metres in length may proceed
explanation: This sign indicates that only vehicles not exceeding 15 metres in overall length may use the road.

text: What instruction does this blue circular sign marked 'AV' give?
options: Animal-drawn vehicles must use this road
Abnormal vehicles must use this road
Agricultural vehicles must use this road
All vehicles must use this road
explanation: The letters 'AV' stand for Abnormal Vehicles. This mandatory sign indicates that abnormal vehicles must use this route.

text: You notice a large white 'X' painted across your lane ahead. What is this road marking warning you about?
options: A railway level crossing is ahead
A no-entry zone begins here
A parking restriction applies
A speed-controlled section starts ahead
explanation: A large white 'X' is an advance road marking warning drivers of an approaching railway level crossing.

text: A regulatory sign shows a bus inside a red circle with a diagonal red bar. Which restriction does this impose?
options: Buses may not enter or continue beyond this point
Only buses may use this road
Bus parking is not permitted
Buses must give way to all other traffic
explanation: A bus symbol crossed by a red diagonal bar prohibits buses from entering or using that section of road.

text: What information is conveyed by a brown tourist-information sign displaying a white fuel pump symbol?
options: Fuel or refuelling facilities are available nearby
Vehicles carrying fuel are prohibited
A vehicle inspection station lies ahead
Emergency fuel is supplied at this location only
explanation: Brown tourist information signs with a fuel pump symbol direct road users to nearby filling stations.

text: What rule applies when you enter an area marked by a red diamond containing a white pedestrian symbol?
options: Pedestrians have priority while vehicle access is restricted and controlled
Only pedestrians may enter; all vehicles are prohibited
The maximum permitted speed is 60 km/h
Motor vehicles have priority over pedestrians
explanation: This sign identifies a pedestrian-priority area where vehicles may be permitted but must yield to pedestrians and comply with the restrictions in place.

text: A blue circular sign displays a headlamp symbol with light rays. What action is required?
options: Switch your vehicle's headlights on
Use parking lights only
Turn off your high-beam headlights
Prepare for a tunnel but keep your lights off until you enter
explanation: Blue circular signs give mandatory instructions. This sign requires drivers to switch on their vehicle's headlights.

text: A goods vehicle carrying a hazardous-material placard appears inside a red circle with a diagonal slash. Which vehicles are affected?
options: Vehicles transporting dangerous goods may not proceed
Only fuel tankers are prohibited
Goods vehicles over 3 500 kg are prohibited
All heavy vehicles are prohibited regardless of their load
explanation: This regulatory sign specifically prohibits vehicles conveying dangerous goods from entering the restricted area.

text: A brown tourist sign features the silhouette of a golfer. What should you understand from this sign?
options: A golf course or golfing facility is located nearby
Watch for golfers crossing the road
A historical attraction is ahead
A sports academy is located on this road
explanation: Brown tourism signs identify places of interest. A golfer symbol indicates a nearby golf course or golfing facility.

text: A red-bordered prohibition sign shows an animal-drawn vehicle crossed by a diagonal bar. What does this indicate?
options: Animal-drawn vehicles are not permitted beyond this point
Horse riders are prohibited
Only motor vehicles may tow trailers here
Animal-drawn vehicles have priority
explanation: The sign prohibits the use of animal-drawn vehicles on that section of road.

text: A brown information sign displays a white cup. What facility is it directing you to?
options: Refreshment facilities such as a café or restaurant
A picnic area only
A hospital with refreshments
A designated parking area
explanation: The cup symbol on a brown tourism sign indicates nearby food or refreshment facilities such as a café or restaurant.

text: A sign depicts a person behind a table and umbrella inside a red circle with a diagonal slash. What restriction is being enforced?
options: Street trading or hawking is prohibited
Markets are permanently closed
Hitchhiking is prohibited
Selling is permitted only with a municipal permit
explanation: This regulatory sign prohibits hawking or street trading in the designated area.

text: A blue circular sign displays a pedestrian on one side and a bicycle on the other. Who is permitted to use the route indicated by this sign?
options: Pedestrians and pedal cyclists only
Pedestrians, cyclists and motorcycles
Cyclists may use the path, but pedestrians should use the roadway
Only non-motorised traffic may use it if separated into different lanes
explanation: A blue circular sign gives a mandatory instruction. This sign indicates that the route is reserved exclusively for pedestrians and pedal cyclists.

text: What is the primary purpose of white lane markings painted between traffic lanes?
options: To separate and guide traffic travelling within designated lanes
To identify areas where parking is permitted
To indicate the maximum speed for the road
To warn drivers that entry is prohibited
explanation: White lane markings separate traffic lanes and help guide vehicles safely along the roadway.

text: A regulatory sign displays '4.42 m' between vertical arrows inside a red border. Which vehicles must obey this restriction?
options: Vehicles taller than 4.42 metres may not continue
Vehicles longer than 4.42 metres are prohibited
Vehicles with a mass exceeding 4.42 tonnes are prohibited
The bridge ahead is exactly 4.42 metres long
explanation: Vertical arrows indicate a height restriction. Vehicles exceeding the displayed height may not proceed.

text: A blue information sign displays a white shopping trolley. What information is it providing?
options: Shopping or retail facilities are available nearby
Delivery vehicles must unload in this area
Shopping trolleys are prohibited beyond this point
Reserved parking for supermarket customers only
explanation: Blue information signs direct road users to services and facilities. The shopping trolley symbol indicates nearby shopping facilities.

text: What instruction is given by a red-bordered sign showing a right-turn arrow crossed by a diagonal bar?
options: Turning right is prohibited
Right turns are compulsory
Yield before turning right
Right turns are permitted only during certain hours
explanation: A crossed-out right-turn arrow is a regulatory sign prohibiting right turns.

text: A blue square sign contains the symbol of a factory. What does this sign indicate?
options: An industrial area or industrial estate is nearby
Heavy goods vehicles must use this route
Industrial hazards exist ahead
Protective clothing is required beyond this point
explanation: This information sign directs road users to an industrial area.

text: A sign shows a bus with the word 'TOUR' above it inside a red-bordered prohibition sign. What restriction applies?
options: Tour buses are prohibited from entering
Only tour buses may continue
Tour buses must stop at this point
Tour buses should expect a sharp bend ahead
explanation: This regulatory sign prohibits tour buses from proceeding beyond the sign.

text: A brown tourist-information sign displays a picnic table. What facility does it identify?
options: A designated picnic site nearby
A public parking area
A rest stop with overnight accommodation
A police assistance point
explanation: Brown tourism signs identify places of interest and facilities. This symbol indicates a picnic area.

text: You encounter a warning sign indicating loose gravel ahead. What is the safest response?
options: Reduce your speed, increase your following distance and drive cautiously
Accelerate to minimise tyre contact with the loose surface
Stop on the shoulder until the gravel section ends
explanation: Loose gravel reduces tyre grip. Slowing down and increasing your following distance improves vehicle control.

text: How can temporary road signs usually be distinguished from permanent traffic signs?
options: They use yellow backgrounds with red and black colouring
They use green backgrounds with white lettering
They are always mounted on orange poles
explanation: Temporary regulatory and warning signs use yellow backgrounds with red and black features to distinguish them from permanent signs.

text: If you see a circular sign with a white background surrounded by a solid red border, what category of regulatory sign is it?
options: A command sign requiring a specific action
A prohibition sign restricting an action or vehicle
A reservation sign identifying a dedicated lane or area
explanation: White circular signs with a red border are prohibition signs. They indicate an action or type of vehicle that is not permitted.

text: Unless another speed limit is displayed, what maximum speed applies when driving within a residential zone?
options: 30 km/h
40 km/h
50 km/h
explanation: Residential zone signs establish a maximum speed limit of 30 km/h unless another speed limit is specifically indicated.

text: A traffic sign is displayed with a red diagonal cross over it. What does this tell you?
options: The restriction or instruction shown on the sign has ended
The restriction now applies only to heavy vehicles
An emergency restriction begins beyond this point
explanation: A red cross over an existing regulatory sign cancels the previous restriction or instruction.

text: Which description correctly identifies a mandatory command sign?
options: A blue circular sign displaying white symbols or numbers
A white circular sign outlined with a red border
A yellow diamond containing black symbols
explanation: Command signs are blue circles with white symbols or numbers, indicating an action that road users must follow.

text: A road sign is shaped as an upward-pointing triangle with a red border. What type of sign are you looking at?
options: A prohibition sign
A warning sign
An information sign
explanation: Warning signs are triangular with red borders and alert drivers to hazards or changing road conditions ahead.

text: If you are permitted to drive into a pedestrian zone, what is the highest speed you may travel?
options: 15 km/h
20 km/h
30 km/h
explanation: Where vehicles are allowed in a pedestrian zone, they must give way to pedestrians and may not exceed 15 km/h.

text: What restriction is indicated by a continuous red line painted along the edge of the kerb?
options: Parking is prohibited, but brief stops are permitted
Stopping is prohibited at all times
The lane is reserved exclusively for buses
explanation: A solid red kerb line indicates a no-stopping zone. You may neither stop nor park there.

text: Blue rectangular signs displaying white symbols or lettering generally indicate what?
options: Tourist attractions or places of interest
Temporary roadworks or detours
Reserved facilities, lanes or areas for specific road users
explanation: Blue rectangular signs are reservation signs used to identify lanes, roads or areas reserved for particular vehicles or users.

text: What does a broken white centre line on a two-way road allow you to do?
options: Cross it only when overtaking or turning can be done safely
Remain on your side because crossing it is never permitted
Use the opposite lane only if you are driving a heavy vehicle
explanation: A broken white centre line may be crossed for overtaking or turning, provided it is safe and legal to do so.

text: You approach a lane marked with the word 'BUS' painted on the road surface. What does this indicate?
options: The lane is reserved for buses and other authorised public transport vehicles
Any vehicle may use the lane outside peak traffic periods
The lane may be used by passenger vehicles when overtaking on the left
explanation: A lane marked 'BUS' identifies a dedicated bus lane that is reserved for authorised public transport vehicles.

text: While travelling on a freeway, you notice a white lane arrow dividing into two directions. What information is this road marking providing?
options: The lane you are travelling in is ending and you must merge immediately
The lane continues but also provides an option to leave the freeway via an upcoming exit
Speed enforcement cameras operate beyond this point
explanation: A bifurcated lane arrow indicates that the lane divides, allowing traffic either to continue ahead or follow an approaching exit.

text: White dashed rectangular blocks painted across the roadway near a pedestrian crossing are intended to identify:
options: A designated crossing area for cyclists where motorists should give way
The start of a raised speed hump
A marked location for speed camera enforcement
explanation: These transverse block markings identify a cyclist crossing associated with the pedestrian crossing.

text: A diagonal white arrow painted in your lane points toward the lane on your left. What action is this marking preparing you for?
options: Prepare to enter a compulsory left-turn lane
Move into the lane indicated because your current lane ends or is obstructed ahead
Leave the roadway by using the left shoulder
explanation: Deflection arrows warn drivers to merge in the direction shown because the current lane will not continue.

text: A solid white line runs alongside a broken white line in the centre of the road. Under which condition may you legally cross these markings?
options: Drivers travelling in either direction may cross whenever it is safe
Only if the broken line is nearest to your lane
Only if the solid line is nearest to your lane
explanation: You may cross a combination line only when the broken line is on your side of the roadway.

text: Before reaching an intersection, you observe several short, broad white transverse lines across your lane. What should you expect?
options: A pedestrian crossing immediately ahead
A series of rumble strips to reduce speed
A yield-controlled intersection where you may need to give right of way
explanation: These markings warn drivers that they are approaching a yield-controlled intersection.

text: What does an inverted white triangle painted on the road surface tell a driver?
options: A high collision-risk area begins ahead
You are approaching a point where you must yield if necessary
The applicable speed limit will reduce shortly
explanation: An inverted white triangle is an advance road marking associated with a yield requirement.

text: A white diamond-shaped road marking is painted in your lane. What is it warning you about?
options: An area reserved exclusively for pedestrians
A section where pedestrians may not walk
A pedestrian crossing further ahead
explanation: This road marking provides advance warning that a pedestrian crossing is approaching.

text: A hazard marker has red and white stripes sloping downward toward the right. How should you pass the obstruction?
options: The obstruction marks the end of the roadway on the right
Keep to the left of the obstruction
Keep to the right of the obstruction
explanation: The direction of the stripes indicates that traffic must pass on the right-hand side of the obstruction.

text: A warning sign shows a truck descending a steep hill. What is the safest response?
options: Select a lower gear before descending to help control your speed
Expect a slippery surface for the next several kilometres
Reduce your speed to the posted minimum limit
explanation: A steep descent warning advises drivers to use a lower gear to maintain control and reduce brake wear.

text: A warning sign depicts water flowing over a dip in the roadway. What hazard is it warning you about?
options: A low-water bridge or drift where water may cover the road surface
A coastal route that is frequently affected by sea spray
A designated vehicle washing point ahead
explanation: This warning sign indicates a drift or low-water crossing where water may flow across the roadway.

text: You see a warning sign showing a vehicle leaving the road and entering water. What hazard is this sign warning you about?
options: Only light motor vehicles may continue beyond this point
An unprotected riverbank, jetty or similar edge where a vehicle could enter the water
The road ahead is used entirely at the driver's own risk
explanation: This warning sign indicates an unprotected riverbank, jetty or similar hazard where extra caution is required.

text: A temporary yellow warning sign displays an arrow making a sharp 90-degree bend to the left. What should you expect?
options: A compulsory left turn at the next intersection
A temporary sharp left-hand deviation or bend ahead because of roadworks
The beginning of a one-way traffic system
explanation: This temporary warning sign alerts drivers to a sharp left deviation or bend ahead, usually associated with roadworks.

text: A warning sign shows stones being thrown up behind a vehicle. What is it warning you about?
options: The sealed road changes to an unpaved surface further ahead
Loose gravel or stones ahead that may reduce grip and cause flying debris
A mountain pass with an uneven road surface
explanation: The sign warns of loose gravel or chippings where vehicles may lose traction and stones can be thrown up.

text: A warning sign shows a thick vertical line crossed by a thinner horizontal line. What information does this provide?
options: You are approaching a major intersection where you automatically have right of way over every vehicle
A minor crossroad joins the main road ahead, so be alert for traffic entering from either side
A hospital access road crosses the route ahead
explanation: The thicker line represents the main road, while the thinner line indicates a minor road intersecting it.

text: What hazard does a warning sign showing rocks falling from a hillside indicate?
options: An unpaved mountain road begins ahead
There is a risk of rocks or debris falling onto the roadway
You are approaching a quarry or mining area
explanation: This sign warns drivers that falling rocks may obstruct the roadway, particularly in mountainous areas.

text: A warning sign displays an aircraft inside a triangle. What should you expect?
options: A military airbase where public vehicles are prohibited
Low-flying aircraft near an airfield that could distract or startle drivers
A compulsory route leading directly to an airport terminal
explanation: The sign warns of aircraft flying at low altitude near an airport or airfield.

text: A warning sign shows two roads crossing each other. What does this indicate?
options: A railway level crossing lies ahead
A crossroad intersection is approaching where traffic may enter from different directions
All vehicles must stop at the next junction
A toll plaza is located ahead
explanation: This warning sign alerts drivers to a crossroads where traffic may approach from either side.

text: A triangular warning sign contains a curved arrow. How should this sign be interpreted?
options: A U-turn must be made ahead
A bend in the road is approaching and your speed should be adjusted accordingly
A one-way side street joins the road ahead
A temporary diversion begins here
explanation: The curved arrow warns of a bend ahead and advises drivers to slow down before entering it.

text: A warning sign displays a sharply curved arrow. What should you anticipate?
options: A gradual series of bends over a long distance
A sharp bend ahead that requires a noticeable reduction in speed
The start of a dual carriageway
Traffic from another lane will merge into yours
explanation: A sharply curved arrow warns drivers of a tight bend where speed should be reduced before entering.

text: When a triangular warning sign displays the symbol of a traffic signal, what should you expect?
options: The traffic lights ahead will remain green
Traffic ahead is controlled only by a traffic officer
Traffic signals are ahead and you should be prepared to stop if required
The traffic lights ahead are permanently out of service
explanation: This warning sign alerts drivers to traffic signals ahead so they can reduce speed and prepare to stop if necessary.

text: You encounter a triangular warning sign showing the silhouette of a pedestrian. What should you anticipate?
options: Pedestrians are not permitted to use or cross the road
People may be walking near or crossing the roadway ahead
Increase your speed to clear the area quickly
A school bus stop is immediately beyond the sign
explanation: This warning sign alerts drivers that pedestrians may be crossing or walking near the road ahead and extra caution is required.

text: A warning sign displays the silhouettes of two children. What is the purpose of this sign?
options: Children may be crossing nearby, so reduce speed and be ready to stop
Parking restrictions apply because of a nearby playground
Children are prohibited from crossing at this location
The road ahead is reserved for pedestrians only
explanation: This warning sign is commonly placed near schools or pedestrian crossings used by children, requiring drivers to take extra care.

text: What does a triangular warning sign containing a bicycle symbol advise motorists?
options: A compulsory cycle lane begins ahead
Cyclists are prohibited beyond this point
Cyclists may be travelling on or crossing the road ahead
A bicycle rental facility is located nearby
explanation: The sign warns drivers to expect cyclists and to adjust their driving accordingly.

text: A triangular warning sign depicts a locomotive with crossing barriers. What does this indicate?
options: A railway level crossing with barriers is ahead where you may need to stop
A railway line that is no longer in use
A railway museum or tourist attraction ahead
A bridge crossing over railway tracks
explanation: This warning sign indicates a controlled railway crossing. Be prepared to stop if warning lights operate or barriers are lowered.

text: When you see a triangular warning sign showing the outline of a tunnel, what should you prepare for?
options: A collapsed section of road ahead
A tunnel where you should switch on your headlights and reduce speed
A bridge with limited overhead clearance
An underpass where changing lanes is prohibited
explanation: This sign warns of a tunnel ahead. Drivers should switch on their headlights and adjust their speed before entering.

text: A warning sign shows a bridge together with a height measurement. What does this information mean?
options: Vehicles taller than the indicated height cannot safely pass underneath
A maximum speed of 40 km/h applies on the bridge
The bridge is narrow and allows only one vehicle at a time
The bridge has a maximum weight restriction
explanation: The sign warns of restricted overhead clearance beneath the structure.

text: A warning sign shows a truck climbing a steep hill. What is the safest driving technique?
options: Select a lower gear before beginning the climb
Accelerate hard to carry maximum speed up the hill
Stop at the bottom of the hill and inspect your brakes
Remain in the highest gear while climbing
explanation: Selecting a lower gear before the ascent helps maintain power and control while climbing a steep gradient.

text: A triangular warning sign illustrates an uneven road surface. What should you expect?
options: You should stop to inspect the road before continuing
Bumps, dips or an uneven road surface ahead requiring a reduction in speed
The road surface changes to gravel shortly
Speed humps continue for the next two kilometres
explanation: This warning sign indicates an uneven road surface where reducing speed will help maintain control and avoid damage.

text: What is a triangular warning sign with a hump-shaped road profile advising you?
options: Speed humps are ahead, so slow down before reaching them
Roadworks with lane closures begin ahead
The sealed road changes to a gravel surface
Road marking maintenance is taking place ahead
explanation: The sign warns of speed humps ahead. Reducing speed helps maintain vehicle control and prevents damage.

text: A warning sign shows a vehicle with wavy skid marks beneath it. What is the intended warning?
options: The road surface ahead may be slippery, requiring smooth steering and gentle braking
Large potholes are present ahead and should be avoided
Water is flowing across the road further ahead
A tyre inspection area is approaching
explanation: This sign warns of a slippery road surface where traction may be reduced. Slow down and avoid sudden steering or braking.

text: A warning sign indicates that the sealed road surface is ending. What should you expect beyond the sign?
options: A gravel road begins where reduced speed and extra caution are advisable
The road is closed to all vehicles except 4x4s
Fresh tar has been laid and the speed limit is 20 km/h
Loose stones are present but your driving speed does not need to change
explanation: This warning sign advises that a gravel road begins ahead. Reduce speed because gravel provides less grip and loose stones may be thrown up by vehicles.

text: You approach the warning sign shown. Which situation should you prepare for?
options: A bridge where opposing vehicles may not be able to pass each other safely
A permanent narrowing of the roadway after the bridge
A bridge carrying a weight restriction only
A bridge where the speed limit increases
explanation: The sign warns of a narrow bridge. Slow down and be prepared to give way if necessary because there may not be enough room for two vehicles.

text: What hazard is this warning sign primarily alerting you to?
options: A speed enforcement camera operating nearby
Emergency or roadwork warning lights ahead requiring you to slow down
An emergency parking bay
Traffic signals that permanently flash amber
explanation: The sign warns that flashing emergency or roadwork lights may be encountered ahead. Reduce speed and be prepared for changing traffic conditions.

text: What is the safest interpretation of this warning sign?
options: Only seasonal flooding affects this road
A low-water drift lies ahead where water may flow across the roadway
A recreational off-road crossing is ahead
The road surface is simply slippery after rain
explanation: This warning sign indicates a drift or low-water crossing. Check the depth and strength of flowing water before attempting to cross.

text: Why should drivers take extra care after seeing this warning sign?
options: Farm machinery has priority over all other traffic
Slow-moving agricultural vehicles may enter or travel on the road ahead
Only tractors are allowed beyond this point
A farm equipment dealership is nearby
explanation: The warning sign indicates that tractors or other agricultural vehicles may be entering, crossing or travelling along the road.

text: If you encounter this warning sign, which driving behaviour is most appropriate?
options: Pass horses quickly before they move into your lane
Reduce speed and pass horses carefully without startling them
Expect a horse racing venue nearby
The road is reserved for horse riders only
explanation: The sign warns of horses or riders ahead. Drive slowly and quietly because sudden noise or speed may frighten the animals.

text: Which action is required when a traffic signal displays a steady red light?
options: Continue if the intersection appears clear
Stop behind the stop line and wait until a green signal is shown
Proceed after sounding your hooter
Treat the signal as a yield sign
explanation: A steady red signal requires you to stop behind the stop line and remain there until the signal changes to green.

text: What does a steady amber (yellow) traffic signal require from a driver who has not yet crossed the stop line?
options: Accelerate before the signal turns red
Stop and wait for the green signal before moving off
Proceed cautiously because you still have right of way
Continue only after checking for pedestrians
explanation: If you have not crossed the stop line when the amber signal appears, you must stop and wait for the green signal. Only vehicles already beyond the stop line should continue.

text: A green traffic signal allows you to proceed, provided that you:
options: Give way to road users already lawfully in the intersection
Drive through immediately without slowing
Stop briefly before entering the junction
Sound your hooter to alert other traffic
explanation: A green signal gives permission to proceed, but you must still yield to pedestrians and vehicles already lawfully within the intersection.

text: An overhead lane control signal displays a steady green arrow above your lane. What does this indicate?
options: The lane is open for traffic travelling in the direction indicated
You must leave the lane before the next intersection
Only emergency vehicles may use the lane
You may use the lane only after signalling
explanation: A steady green arrow above a lane indicates that the lane is open for normal travel in the direction shown.

text: If an overhead lane control signal above your lane displays a steady red cross (✕), what are you required to do?
options: Use the lane only if traffic is moving slowly
Do not drive in that lane under any circumstances
Enter the lane only to stop in an emergency
Treat the lane as reserved for buses and taxis
explanation: A steady red cross indicates that the lane is closed to traffic. Drivers must not enter or continue travelling in that lane.

text: What does a steady green pedestrian signal ('green man') indicate?
options: Pedestrians may cross within the marked crossing while vehicles must give way
Pedestrians should wait because vehicles have priority
Only children are permitted to cross
Drivers may continue without considering pedestrians
explanation: A steady green pedestrian signal gives pedestrians permission to cross. Drivers must yield to pedestrians lawfully using the crossing.

text: A flashing red pedestrian signal is displayed. Which statement is correct?
options: Anyone who has not yet stepped onto the crossing must wait
Pedestrians should stop immediately, even if already crossing
Drivers may accelerate through the crossing
The pedestrian signal is faulty and should be ignored
explanation: A flashing red pedestrian signal means new pedestrians must not begin crossing. Those already on the crossing should finish crossing safely.

text: You are facing a traffic officer who has one arm raised vertically with the palm facing you. What instruction applies to you?
options: Proceed straight through the intersection
Stop and wait until directed otherwise
Only traffic behind the officer must stop
Turn at the nearest opportunity
explanation: When a traffic officer faces you with a raised palm, traffic approaching from that direction must stop.

text: A traffic officer extends an arm towards you and repeatedly motions you forward. How should you interpret this signal?
options: Pull over immediately for inspection
Reverse until instructed to stop
Continue forward as directed
Remain stationary until the officer lowers both arms
explanation: A forward beckoning motion instructs traffic facing the officer to proceed through the controlled area.

text: A white sign displaying a yellow diamond informs drivers that:
options: You are travelling on a priority road
You must yield at every upcoming intersection
A toll road begins ahead
Private vehicles are prohibited
explanation: The yellow diamond indicates a priority road where you have right of way over traffic entering from minor roads, unless another sign or signal states otherwise.

text: What information is conveyed by a green rectangular sign displaying a stylised white 'i'?
options: A tourist or travel information centre is available nearby
An immigration control point lies ahead
An insurance office is located nearby
A road incident has occurred ahead
explanation: This guidance sign directs road users to an information centre where travel or tourist assistance is available.

text: What is the purpose of the sequence of boards marked 300 m, 200 m and 100 m before a junction or hazard?
options: They identify a speed camera enforcement zone
They provide a countdown to an upcoming hazard or junction
They indicate fuel-saving checkpoints
They show the road's kilometre reference points
explanation: Countdown boards give advance notice of the remaining distance to a hazard, junction or other important feature.

text: A continuous white line separates traffic travelling in opposite directions. What does this road marking mean?
options: You may cross it only when overtaking slow vehicles
Crossing or straddling the line to overtake is prohibited
Only vehicles on the right-hand side may cross it
The marking is advisory rather than compulsory
explanation: A solid centre line indicates that vehicles must not cross or straddle it to overtake.

text: Chevron road markings form a painted island in the roadway. Which statement is correct?
options: You may enter the painted area when traffic is congested
The hatched area is not intended for vehicles to drive on
The markings identify a cycle lane
Vehicles must stop within the chevrons before turning
explanation: Chevron markings outline a painted traffic island used to channel traffic. Vehicles should not drive on or across the hatched area.

text: A vehicle is parked within a white rectangular outline painted on the road surface. What does this marking designate?
options: An emergency stopping area
A designated parking bay for one vehicle
A loading zone reserved for delivery vehicles
A no-stopping area
explanation: The rectangular outline marks an official parking bay where a vehicle should be parked within the marked boundaries.

text: White zig-zag markings are painted on the road leading up to a pedestrian crossing. What do they indicate?
options: Parking and overtaking are prohibited near the crossing
A speed hump is immediately ahead
Drivers must change lanes before the crossing
A railway crossing is approaching
explanation: These markings keep the crossing visible by prohibiting parking and overtaking on the approach.

text: What restriction applies where a continuous yellow line is painted along the kerb?
options: Parking is limited during certain hours
Stopping alongside the kerb is prohibited
You may stop briefly to drop off passengers
Only buses may stop there
explanation: A solid yellow kerb line indicates a no-stopping zone.

text: You notice a large white 'X' painted across the road at railway tracks. What should you understand from this marking?
options: You are approaching a railway crossing where you must stop and check before crossing
Cross the railway tracks without hesitation
Sound your hooter while crossing
Only stop if warning lights are operating
explanation: The X-shaped marking reinforces that a railway level crossing is ahead and drivers must exercise caution before crossing.

text: A white inverted triangle is painted in your lane. What is it warning you about?
options: A speed camera lies ahead
A yield point is approaching where you may need to stop and give way
A turning lane begins ahead
A pedestrian crossing starts here
explanation: The inverted triangle is an advance warning that you are approaching a yield sign or yield line.

text: Arrow-shaped road markings direct traffic from two lanes into one. What action should you take?
options: Prepare for a lower speed limit
Merge safely into the indicated lane before your lane ends
Move into a newly opened lane
Prepare to turn right from your lane
explanation: These lane reduction arrows warn that your lane is ending and you must merge safely.

text: Broken white guidance lines continue through an intersection. What is their purpose?
options: They indicate that overtaking is compulsory
They guide drivers along the correct path through the intersection or curve
They mark a reserved taxi lane
They indicate where pedestrians should wait
explanation: Guidance lines help road users follow the correct path through complex intersections and curves.

text: Which motorcycle control is primarily used to steer and change the direction of travel?
options: The clutch lever and gear lever
The handlebars
The rear brake pedal
explanation: The handlebars are used to steer and control the motorcycle's direction.

text: Before turning left or right on a motorcycle, which control should you operate to inform other road users?
options: The throttle
The direction indicator switch
The headlight beam switch
explanation: The direction indicator switch signals your intention to turn.

text: Which motorcycle control must be operated to disconnect engine power while changing gears?
options: The clutch lever
The front brake lever
The gear lever
explanation: The clutch lever disengages the engine from the transmission to allow smooth gear changes.

text: When braking a motorcycle under normal riding conditions, which method provides the safest and most effective stopping performance?
options: Apply only the front brake lever
Apply both the front brake lever and the rear brake pedal together
Switch off the engine using the kill switch
explanation: Maximum controlled braking is achieved by using both the front and rear brakes together in a smooth, balanced manner.

text: In an emergency, what is the purpose of the motorcycle's engine cut-off (kill) switch?
options: To change to the fuel reserve supply
To immediately stop the engine by cutting the ignition
To disengage the transmission from the engine
explanation: The engine cut-off switch instantly stops the engine without requiring you to remove your hands from the handlebars.

text: Which motorcycle control is used to increase engine speed while riding?
options: The left-hand grip
The right-hand twist-grip throttle
The gear shift lever
explanation: Rotating the right-hand twist-grip throttle increases engine speed and accelerates the motorcycle.

text: When stopped on an uphill slope, which control should you use to prevent the motorcycle from rolling backwards before moving off?
options: The clutch lever
The rear brake pedal
The kick starter
explanation: Using the rear brake keeps the motorcycle stationary while allowing you to coordinate the clutch and throttle for a smooth uphill start.

text: Which motorcycle control is used to select different gears while riding?
options: The front brake lever
The left-foot gear selector lever
The fuel tap
explanation: Motorcycles use a foot-operated gear selector on the left side to change gears.

text: On the motorcycle controls diagram, which numbered control operates the rear brake?
explanation: Control number 7 represents the foot-operated rear brake pedal.

text: According to the motorcycle controls diagram, which numbered control is used to accelerate?
explanation: Control number 5 is the twist-grip throttle used to increase engine speed.

text: Which numbered control on the motorcycle is used to increase or decrease engine speed?
explanation: Control number 5 is the throttle, which regulates engine speed.

text: Which combination of numbered controls must be used together when changing gears on a motorcycle?
options: 1 and 2
2 and 9
4 and 7
explanation: Changing gears requires the clutch (1) and the gear selector (2) to be used together.

text: Which numbered control on the motorcycle is primarily used for steering and changing direction?
explanation: Control number 8 represents the handlebars, which are used to steer the motorcycle.

text: Which numbered control on the motorcycle diagram is used to signal your intention to turn or change direction?
explanation: Control number 6 is the direction indicator switch, used to signal your intentions to other road users.

text: Which combination of numbered controls should be used together to bring a motorcycle to a controlled stop?
options: 4 and 7
4 and 9
2 and 5
explanation: Controls 4 and 7 represent the front brake and rear brake, which should normally be applied together for effective braking.

text: Which group of numbered controls is used to regulate and control the speed of a motorcycle while riding?
options: 2, 3 and 5
4, 5 and 7
5, 6 and 9
explanation: The throttle (5), front brake (4) and rear brake (7) are the primary controls used to increase, reduce and regulate speed.

text: Which numbered control regulates the amount of fuel and power delivered to the motorcycle's engine?
explanation: Control number 5 is the twist-grip throttle, which regulates engine power and speed.

text: Which numbered control is operated to disengage the engine from the gearbox before changing gears?
explanation: Control number 2 represents the clutch lever, which disconnects the engine from the gearbox during gear changes.

text: Which numbered control is used to select a different gear on the motorcycle?
explanation: Control number 1 is the foot-operated gear selector used to change gears.

text: Immediately before bringing a motorcycle to a complete stop, which combination of numbered controls should be used?
options: 1, 3 and 5
4, 6 and 9
2, 4 and 7
explanation: The clutch (2), front brake (4) and rear brake (7) are used together when coming to a complete stop.

text: Which numbered control allows you to observe traffic approaching from behind while riding?
explanation: Control number 3 represents the rear-view mirror, used to monitor traffic behind you.

text:
When using controls 4 and 7 to brake, which of the following will increase your stopping distance?
(i) Carrying a passenger.
(ii) Riding on a wet road.
(iii) Travelling at a higher speed.
options: (i) and (iii) only
(i), (ii) and (iii)
(ii) and (iii) only
explanation: Stopping distance increases when carrying extra weight, riding on a wet surface and travelling at higher speeds.

text: According to the motorcycle controls diagram, which combination of numbered controls would typically be used when negotiating a tight turn?
explanation: A tight turn requires coordinated use of the clutch, gear selector, mirror, throttle, rear brake and handlebars to maintain balance and control.

text: Which control is used to select the required gear, including reverse, in a manual transmission vehicle?
options: The hazard warning switch
The gear lever
The handbrake lever
explanation: The gear lever is used to select the appropriate gear, including reverse, in a manual transmission vehicle.

text: Which two controls must be coordinated when changing gears smoothly in a manual vehicle?
options: The handbrake and the brake pedal
The clutch pedal and the accelerator pedal
The brake pedal and the accelerator pedal
explanation: Smooth gear changes require proper coordination of the clutch pedal and accelerator pedal.

text: Before moving off from the side of the road, how should you check your blind spot?
options: Rely only on your mirrors
Turn your head and look over your shoulder
Switch on your hazard lights first
explanation: Mirrors do not show every area around the vehicle. A shoulder check is necessary to ensure your blind spot is clear.

text: Which control should always be applied to help prevent a parked vehicle from rolling on a slope?
options: The foot brake
The handbrake
Leaving the vehicle in neutral
explanation: The handbrake secures the vehicle and helps prevent it from rolling when parked.

text: Which control should you use to warn other road users of immediate danger?
options: The hazard light switch
The hooter (horn)
The windscreen washer switch
explanation: The hooter is used to provide an audible warning when necessary to avoid danger.

text: Which control allows you to steer the vehicle through bends and keep it in its lane?
options: The steering wheel
The dashboard dimmer control
The indicator stalk
explanation: The steering wheel controls the direction of the front wheels.

text: Which control must be operated before turning or changing lanes to inform other road users of your intention?
options: The headlight switch
The indicator stalk
The hazard warning switch
explanation: The indicator stalk activates the direction indicators to signal your intentions.

text: Which pedal is not fitted to a vehicle with an automatic transmission?
options: The clutch pedal
The brake pedal
The accelerator pedal
explanation: Automatic vehicles do not have a clutch pedal because gear changes are performed automatically.

text: Which pedal is primarily used to slow down or stop a vehicle during normal driving?
options: The handbrake
The clutch pedal
The foot brake pedal
explanation: The foot brake pedal operates the vehicle's service braking system.

text: Before braking or changing lanes, which mirrors should you check to assess traffic behind you?
options: The interior rear-view mirror and both side mirrors
Only the vanity mirror
The windscreen only
explanation: Checking the rear-view mirror together with both side mirrors gives you the best awareness of traffic behind and alongside your vehicle.

text: Which control should be activated first to maintain clear forward visibility when heavy rain suddenly reduces your vision?
options: The windscreen wiper control
The main-beam headlight switch
The demister fan control
explanation: The windscreen wipers remove rain from the windscreen, allowing you to maintain a clear view of the road.

text: Which control regulates the amount of fuel supplied to the engine and therefore controls the vehicle's speed?
options: The clutch pedal
The accelerator pedal
The gear lever
explanation: The accelerator pedal controls engine power and allows you to increase or maintain your driving speed.

text: If your vehicle becomes a hazard to other road users, which control activates all four indicator lights simultaneously?
options: The indicator stalk
The hazard warning switch
The ignition switch
explanation: The hazard warning switch flashes all four indicators together to warn approaching traffic of a stationary or slow-moving hazard.

text: When changing to a lower gear in a manual vehicle, which pedal must be depressed before moving the gear lever?
options: The handbrake
The clutch pedal
The foot brake
explanation: Pressing the clutch disconnects the engine from the gearbox, allowing gears to be changed smoothly.

text: When another vehicle approaches at night, which control is used to switch from main beam to dipped beam?
options: The hazard warning switch
The headlight dimmer (beam selector) switch
The ignition switch
explanation: The beam selector switch changes between main and dipped headlights to avoid dazzling oncoming drivers.

text: Which numbered control would you NOT expect to find in an automatic transmission vehicle?
explanation: Automatic vehicles do not have a clutch pedal.

text: If you need to check traffic approaching from the right rear of your vehicle, which numbered control should you use?
explanation: Number 3 represents the right-hand exterior mirror, which is used to monitor traffic behind and to the right of the vehicle.

text: Which numbered control is used when increasing the speed of your vehicle?
explanation: Number 10 represents the accelerator pedal, which controls the vehicle's speed.

text: When leaving your vehicle parked, which numbered control helps prevent it from rolling away?
explanation: Number 7 represents the parking brake (handbrake), which secures the vehicle when parked.

text: Which combination of numbered controls is used when changing gears in a manual transmission vehicle?
options: A. 8 and 10
B. 8 and 6
C. 8 and 7
explanation: Changing gears requires using the clutch pedal together with the gear lever.

text: Which numbered control is used to bring your vehicle to a stop during normal driving?
explanation: Number 9 represents the foot brake, which is used to slow down and stop the vehicle.

text:
When braking, which of the following conditions will increase your vehicle's stopping distance?
(i) Carrying passengers.
(ii) Driving on a wet road.
(iii) Travelling at a higher speed.
options: A. (i) only
B. (ii) and (iii) only
C. (i), (ii) and (iii)
explanation: Stopping distance increases when the vehicle is carrying additional weight, the road surface is wet, and when travelling at higher speeds.

text: Which numbered control must be used when engaging or disengaging gears in a manual vehicle?
explanation: Number 8 represents the clutch pedal, which disconnects the engine from the gearbox during gear changes.

text: Which numbered control is used to increase the speed of the vehicle?
explanation: Number 10 represents the accelerator pedal, which increases engine power and vehicle speed.

text: Which numbered control is used to indicate your intention to turn or change direction?
explanation: Number 5 represents the indicator control, which signals your intention to turn or change lanes.

text: Which numbered control should be used to warn other road users of immediate danger and help avoid a collision?
explanation: Number 11 represents the hooter (horn), which is used to warn other road users of immediate danger.

text: Just before bringing a manual vehicle to a complete stop, which numbered control should be operated to prevent the engine from stalling?
explanation: Number 8 is the clutch pedal. Depressing it before the vehicle stops completely prevents the engine from stalling.

text: Which numbered control is used to clean the windscreen while driving?
explanation: Number 2 represents the windscreen wiper control, which clears rain and debris from the windscreen.

text: Which combination of numbered controls would typically be used when negotiating a tight turn safely?
options: B. 1, 4, 6, 8, 9 and 10 only
explanation: Making a tight turn requires coordinating steering, clutch, brakes, accelerator and observation controls.

text: When changing gears in a heavy motor vehicle with a manual transmission, which control must be depressed to disconnect the engine from the gearbox?
options: The gear selector
The clutch pedal
The parking brake control
explanation: The clutch pedal disconnects the engine from the transmission, allowing gears to be selected or changed smoothly.

text: Which instrument allows the driver of a heavy motor vehicle to monitor engine revolutions per minute (RPM)?
options: The speedometer
The tachometer (rev counter)
The air pressure gauge
explanation: The tachometer displays engine RPM, helping the driver select the correct gear and avoid over-revving the engine.

text: Before driving a heavy vehicle fitted with air brakes, which gauge should always indicate sufficient pressure for safe braking?
options: The engine oil pressure gauge
The air pressure gauge
The transmission temperature gauge
explanation: The air pressure gauges confirm that the braking system has built up enough pressure for safe operation.

text: Which control is used to change between the low and high gear ranges on a heavy vehicle transmission?
options: The steering adjustment lever
The gear range selector switch
The tachograph control
explanation: The gear range selector changes the transmission between low and high gear ranges on heavy vehicles.

text: When descending a long, steep hill, which system helps slow the vehicle without excessive use of the service brakes?
options: The parking brake
The engine brake (retarder/exhaust brake)
The differential lock
explanation: The engine brake or retarder reduces speed on long descents and helps prevent the service brakes from overheating.

text: Which control should be applied to secure an air-brake-equipped heavy vehicle when it is parked?
options: The engine retarder control
The parking brake control
The gear range selector
explanation: Applying the parking brake locks the vehicle in place and prevents it from moving while parked.

text: Which control is primarily used to steer a heavy vehicle through corners and intersections?
options: The steering wheel
The trailer brake lever
The differential lock control
explanation: The steering wheel controls the direction of the vehicle by turning the front wheels.

text: Which control should be used to indicate your intention to turn left or right while driving a heavy vehicle?
options: The windscreen wiper control
The indicator switch
The headlight flash control
explanation: The indicator switch signals your intention to turn or change lanes, giving other road users advance warning.

text: If a heavy vehicle becomes a slow-moving hazard on a steep incline, which control should be activated to warn other road users?
options: The tachograph control
The hazard warning light switch
The air horn
explanation: The hazard warning lights flash all indicators simultaneously to warn approaching traffic of a potential hazard.

text: Which control is used to regulate engine power and increase the speed of a heavy vehicle?
options: The service brake pedal
The accelerator pedal
The engine brake switch
explanation: The accelerator pedal controls the amount of fuel supplied to the engine and therefore regulates the vehicle's speed.

text: Which control is used to slow down or stop a heavy vehicle during normal driving?
options: The retarder control
The service brake pedal
The trailer emergency air valve
explanation: The service brake pedal operates the vehicle's primary braking system to reduce speed or bring the vehicle to a stop.

text: When driving a truck, which mirrors should be checked continuously to monitor the trailer and traffic behind you?
options: The exterior side mirrors
Only the interior rear-view mirror
The sun visor mirrors
explanation: Because the load or trailer blocks the rear window, truck drivers rely mainly on the exterior side mirrors to monitor surrounding traffic.

text: If mud or spray from other vehicles reduces your visibility, which controls should you use to clear the windscreen?
options: The windscreen wiper and washer controls
The main-beam headlight switch
The cab ventilation controls
explanation: Using the windscreen wipers together with the washer system helps remove dirt and spray, restoring clear visibility.

text: Which control must be pressed before changing gears in a manual heavy vehicle?
options: The parking brake control
The clutch pedal
The service brake pedal
explanation: Pressing the clutch pedal disconnects the engine from the gearbox, allowing smooth gear changes.

text: When meeting oncoming traffic at night, which control is used to switch from main beam to dipped beam?
options: The hazard warning switch
The headlight dimmer (beam selector) switch
The ignition switch
explanation: The headlight dimmer switch changes between main and dipped beams to avoid dazzling oncoming drivers.

text: Which numbered control assists the driver when changing gears?
explanation: Number 8 represents the clutch, which is used together with the gear lever when changing gears.

text: Which numbered control changes the direction in which the vehicle travels?
explanation: Number 4 represents the steering wheel, which is used to steer the vehicle.

text: Which numbered control should be applied to prevent your parked vehicle from moving?
explanation: Number 7 represents the parking brake (handbrake), which keeps the vehicle stationary when parked.

text: After parking, which numbered control is used to keep the vehicle stationary?
explanation: Number 7 is the parking brake, which secures the vehicle after it has been parked.

text: Which numbered control is used to slow down or stop the vehicle?
explanation: Number 9 represents the service brake pedal, which is used to reduce speed or stop the vehicle.

text: Which numbered control is used to increase the vehicle's speed?
explanation: Number 10 represents the accelerator pedal, which increases the vehicle's speed.

text: Which numbered control must be operated to indicate your intention to turn left or right?
explanation: Control 5 operates the direction indicators, warning other road users of your intention to turn or change direction.

text: While driving in heavy rain, which numbered control should you use to maintain a clear view through the windscreen?
explanation: Control 2 operates the windscreen wipers, helping maintain visibility during rain.

text: To observe traffic approaching from the right rear of your heavy vehicle, which numbered control should you use?
explanation: Control 3 is the right-hand exterior mirror, used to monitor traffic on the vehicle's right-hand side.

text: Which numbered controls must be used together when changing gears in a manual heavy motor vehicle?
options: A. 8 and 6
B. 8 and 9
C. 8 and 10
explanation: Control 8 (clutch) and Control 6 (gear lever) are used together to change gears smoothly.

text: If you need to reduce your vehicle's speed under normal driving conditions, which numbered control should you apply?
explanation: Control 9 is the service brake, which is used to slow down or stop the vehicle.

text: The stopping distance of a heavy vehicle becomes longer when it is: (i) Carrying a load. (ii) Travelling on a wet road. (iii) Travelling at a higher speed.
options: A. (i) only
B. (ii) and (iii) only
C. (i), (ii) and (iii)
explanation: A heavier load, wet road surface and higher speed all increase the distance required to bring a heavy vehicle to a stop.

text: When stopping on a steep incline to prevent your heavy vehicle from rolling backwards, which numbered control should you use?
explanation: Control 7 is the parking brake (handbrake), which prevents the vehicle from rolling when stationary on a slope.

text: Which numbered controls allow you to monitor both the centre rear view and the right-hand rear view of your vehicle?
options: A. 10 and 5
B. 3 and 1
C. 3 and 2
explanation: Control 3 is the right-hand exterior mirror and Control 1 is the interior rear-view mirror, allowing you to monitor both areas behind the vehicle.

text: To negotiate a tight turn safely in a heavy vehicle, which combination of numbered controls would typically be used?
explanation: Making a tight turn requires using the mirrors, steering wheel, gear controls, clutch, brake and accelerator together to maintain full control of the vehicle.
